name: Q1472733
description: American jazz clarinetist, saxophonist, arranger, and composer (1906–1957)
abstract: Joseph Anthony "Fud" Livingston (April 10, 1906 – March 25, 1957) was an American jazz clarinetist, saxophonist, arranger, and composer.

about_quotes.speaker: Bud Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: Fud Livingston was leading the band and they wanted a tenor man right away... I got into the Pollack band... Fud Livingston was leading the band

about_quotes.speaker: Spiegle Willcox and Al Gallodoro
about_quotes.evidence: Except names like Bix Beiderbecke. And Trumbauer, and Fud Livingston played a little while. Well you know these names.
about_quotes.doc_title: Spiegle Willcox and Al Gallodoro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Norwich, New York, November 14, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Chauncey Morehouse
about_quotes.evidence: With the All Star orchestra, with Miff Moll and Dudley Fosdick, Fud Livingston, Don Murray, Joe Venuti.

about_quotes.speaker: Wild Bill Davison
about_quotes.evidence: Fud Livingston made a lot of arrangements for me while I was in the Army.
